# ARC-QUIC-CRT-0038 - QUIC client-side peer early-data disposition architecture

## Purpose

Describe how the managed client/runtime path observes peer EncryptedExtensions early-data disposition, surfaces accepted versus rejected disposition, discards dormant ZeroRtt packet-protection material on rejection, and keeps accepted disposition intact without widening public early-data support or claiming anti-replay.

## Requirements Satisfied

- REQ-QUIC-CRT-0143

## Design Summary

The TLS transcript-progress path parses EncryptedExtensions and detects the peer early_data extension on the PSK-capable branch. The bridge driver publishes the resulting disposition as a dedicated state update, and the bridge state stores that disposition only after accepted resumption has already been established. When the disposition is rejected, the bridge state reuses the existing key-discard helper to remove only dormant ZeroRtt packet-protection material. Accepted dispositions leave the dormant material intact, and malformed or duplicated early_data data fails closed through the existing fatal/update path.

## Key Components

- src/Incursa.Quic/QuicConnectionRuntime.cs
- src/Incursa.Quic/QuicTlsTransportBridgeDriver.cs
- src/Incursa.Quic/QuicTlsTranscriptProgress.cs
- src/Incursa.Quic/QuicTlsTransport.cs
- src/Incursa.Quic/QuicTransportTlsBridgeState.cs
- tests/Incursa.Quic.Tests/RequirementHomes/CRT/QuicPostHandshakeTicketTestSupport.cs
- tests/Incursa.Quic.Tests/RequirementHomes/CRT/REQ-QUIC-CRT-0143.cs

## Edge Cases and Constraints

- Client role only.
- PSK-capable EncryptedExtensions only.
- Zero-length early_data means accepted disposition.
- Missing early_data means rejected disposition.
- Malformed or duplicated early_data data must fail closed through the fatal/update path.
- Rejected dispositions discard only dormant ZeroRtt material.
- Accepted dispositions leave dormant ZeroRtt material intact.
- The slice does not add 0-RTT receive handling, server admission, key update, transfer, retry, public API widening, or anti-replay support.

## Alternatives Considered

- Treat absence of early_data as accepted. Rejected because the requirement needs an explicit rejection boundary that can discard dormant ZeroRtt material.
- Clear all packet-protection material on rejection. Rejected because the cleanup must not disturb unrelated handshake or 1-RTT material.

## Risks

- If the parser accepts malformed or duplicated early_data, the client can misclassify peer disposition and leave stale dormant state behind.
- If the cleanup is implemented only in the runtime, direct bridge-state callers can retain stale ZeroRtt material after rejection.
- If the slice is widened into public early-data admission, it overclaims support instead of staying an internal disposition observation.
